your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
May need to adapt the wstat formula when using the reflected regions background method to incorporate uncertainties on counts_of by including the standard deviation of the individual counts_off by regions.


**Describe the solution you'd like**
Implementation of the Li and Ma equivalent for a modeled background (see [Vianello 2018](https://doi.org/10.3847/1538-4365/aab780) section 3.4) 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
/

**Additional context**
If my understanding is correct, the current implementation of wstat for the reflected regions background is not entirely accurate and one should also take into account possible effects by averaging over multiple regions.

By setting the acceptance_off to [len(regions_off)](https://github.com/gammapy/gammapy/blob/main/gammapy/makers/background/reflected.py#L563) and acceptance to [1](https://github.com/gammapy/gammapy/blob/main/gammapy/makers/background/reflected.py#L583), $\alpha$ is the arithmetic mean for counts_off. This is then the equivalent of "modeling" the background as described in the paper and equation 15 should be applied.
The same is done e.g. in [3ML](https://github.com/threeML/threeML/blob/master/threeML/utils/statistics/stats_tools.py#L323)

If you think this is something one should take care of I am open to drafting a first PR :)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/issues/5642/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/issues/5642/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
